items.title: Single-nucleus epigenomic profiling of the adult human central nervous system unveils epigenetic memory of developmental programs
items.author: Gonçalo Castelo-Branco
items.content: Single-nucleus epigenomic maps of the adult human brain and spinal cord reveal that adult oligodendroglia retain developmental chromatin patterns, suggesting a molecular memory that may shape repair processes and cancer vulnerability.

items.title: Cortical regulation of collective social dynamics during environmental challenge
items.author: Weizhe Hong
items.content: Raam, Li, Gu and colleagues identify neural mechanisms underlying group huddling in mice during cold exposure. They find that the prefrontal cortex encodes decisions to huddle and that silencing neural activity in some animals causes partners to compensate in response.

items.title: Reduced mediodorsal thalamus activity underlies aberrant belief dynamics in a genetic mouse model of schizophrenia
items.author: Guoping Feng
items.content: Zhou, Ho and colleagues show that, in mice, a schizophrenia-associated mutation impairs belief updating by weakening mediodorsal (MD) thalamus activity. Boosting this activity restored flexible decision-making, suggesting MD thalamus as a potential therapeutic target.

items.title: Mapping deep brain stimulation-modulated circuits via precision neuroimaging
items.content: Precision neuroimaging of repeatedly scanned individuals receiving deep brain stimulation (DBS), conducted longitudinally with long scanning durations, revealed separate globus pallidus and motor cortex (M1) circuits with distinct frequency- and time-dependent responses. DBS also evoked divergent effects in M1 functional connectivity, normalizing within the somato-cognitive action network but denormalizing in effector motor networks.

items.title: Organization of neuropeptide systems in the human brain
items.author: Bratislav Misic
items.content: This study comprehensively maps neuropeptide systems in the human brain to elucidate their organizational principles and shows how neuropeptide architecture is linked to behavior and evolution.

items.title: Restoring rapid natural bimanual typing with a neuroprosthesis after paralysis
items.author: Daniel B. Rubin
items.content: Jude et al. present a communication intracortical brain–computer interface typing neuroprosthesis enabling familiar, high-speed, bimanual QWERTY keyboard functionality for people with paralysis through the decoding of attempted finger movements.

items.title: Circuit response to neuromodulation characterized with simultaneous deep brain stimulation and precision neuroimaging in humans
items.author: Hesheng Liu
items.content: Using a 3-T MRI-compatible deep brain stimulation (DBS) system, Ren at al. densely sampled longitudinal, multimodal neuroimaging data from patients with Parkinson’s disease across multiple stimulation conditions spanning a year, mapping circuit-specific DBS-induced responses.

items.title: Deconstruction of a memory engram reveals distinct ensembles recruited at learning
items.author: Gisella Vetere
items.content: Pouget et al. identified distinct CA1 neuron ensembles active during specific moments of fear learning and uncovered the core engram essential for memory formation.
